title: phyllostegia stachyoides
intro: Phyllostegia stachyoides, also known as Hawaiian lilac or ma'ohina, is a flowering plant native to the Hawaiian Islands, characterized by its fragrant, purple flowers and woody, shrub-like appearance. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Phyllostegia stachyoides include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and exhibit antioxidant properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, salves, and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, respiratory issues, and digestive problems.
